items.title: Trump to slap 'sweeping' new tariffs on 60 trade partners as global duties expire
items.summary: The Trump administration has ramped its tariff actions back up since the president's trade  agenda suffered major legal setbacks earlier this year.

items.title: Intel's stock jumps as chipmaker rides AI boom to fastest revenue growth in almost 15 years
items.summary: Intel rallied after the company reported blowout earnings and guidance.

items.title: Pentagon lowers official Iran war death toll, omitting four killed this month
items.summary: The Pentagon lowered its official Iran war death toll to 14 from 18, removing four service members killed this month.

items.title: Trump's new global tariff draws rebukes from trade partners over forced-labor justification
items.summary: U.S. trading partners have rejected the forced-labor rationale behind Trump's new global tariffs, while most signaled plans to keep negotiating.

items.title: Treasury yields retreat, 10-year hovers around January 2025 highs
items.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for mortgage and auto loans and credit card debt — was last seen down one basis point at 4.693%.

items.title: Russian attack cuts power to around 150,000 in Ukraine’s north as Kyiv hits missile, oil facilities
items.summary: The update comes as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy welcomed strikes on a key missile components facility in the Russian city of Kirov.

items.title: Oracle signs 10-year software contract with Pentagon worth up to $7 billion
items.summary: Oracle will supply on-premises software to the U.S. Department of Defense.

items.title: Amazon cracks down on use of AI images by sellers after New York law
items.summary: A recently enacted New York law mandates that companies disclose when an ad includes a "synthetic performer" in place of a human actor.

items.title: Boom in tech wealth is fueling record prices for dinosaur bones, art and watches, auction houses say
items.summary: The major auction houses racked up nearly $10 billion in sales in the first half, marking one of the strongest-ever starts to the year.

items.title: As Honda CR-V leads U.S. sales, automaker teases new American-built pickup truck
items.summary: The future of the Ridgeline midsize pickup truck had been in flux, and the automaker said there will be a temporary production stoppage for the truck.

items.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
items.summary: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

items.title: OpenAI's Hugging Face hack triggers 'AI Kill Switch' bill in Congress
items.summary: OpenAI disclosed this week that some of its AI models went rogue and hacked into open-source developer platform Hugging Face.

items.title: Southwest Airlines put Texas jet fuel on a boat to LA for the first time amid supply worries
items.summary: Southwest hired a ship this spring to send fuel from Houston to Los Angeles in case of supply shortages.

items.title: U.S., other nations back open-source AI with 'strong security' at China summit
items.summary: The APEC statement is the first to include open-source cooperation at a minister level, said Li Lecheng, China's industry and information technology minister.

items.title: Tesla, Alphabet lose hundreds of billions in value in post-earnings stock plunge
items.summary: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: Intel's turnaround under CEO Lip-Bu Tan gains steam with another strong quarter
items.summary: It's hard not to be impressed by Tan, who, in a little more than a year, has transformed the company.

items.title: Volkswagen CFO addresses plant closures, job losses as profits sink
items.summary: German auto giant Volkswagen on Friday reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter profit and revised down its sales forecast for 2026.

items.title: South Korea's president invokes dramatic Japan realty crash to push domestic property agenda
items.summary: Lee was highlighting the country's excessive investment in real estate, and highlighted that Japan's housing market "burst like a balloon."

items.title: U.S. to see higher generic drug prices thanks to tariffs, CEO of leading India pharma firm warns
items.summary: CEO of Indian pharma company Dr Reddy's has warned that Trump's proposed tariffs on generic drugs will make them more expensive for patients.

items.title: Japan core inflation rate in June creeps up from 4-year low as higher oil prices bite
items.summary: This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.

items.title: China's largest memory chipmaker sparks fears of a cash drain as it readies for public debut
items.summary: CXMT is stoking fears that its blockbuster stock market debut could pull cash from China's equities.

items.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
items.summary: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

items.title: Allianz to buy HSBC’s Singapore insurance unit for $2.09 billion
items.summary: Allianz to acquire Singapore insurance business from HSBC

items.title: Congress splits on war powers resolutions to force Trump to abandon Iran war
items.summary: Trump would be nearly certain to veto any measure to curtail his ability to wage war with Iran.

items.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
items.summary: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

items.title: Cerebras stock gains on AMD partnership
items.summary: Cerebras and AMD agreed to work together, pairing their technology for AI systems.

items.title: DOJ withdraws subpoenas to NY Times reporters in Trump plane probe
items.summary: The subpoenas to New York Times reporters were issued by Manhattan U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton, whom Trump has nominated as director of national intelligence.

items.title: IBM's Krishna tries to reassure investors that AI won't disrupt company's software unit
items.summary: IBM's CEO highlighted growing mainframe hardware capacity, and said software should "catch back up" within a year.

items.title: A tax break for preserving land has drawn IRS scrutiny. Here’s when it still makes sense
items.summary: For wealthy landowners, conservation easements can still make sense if you avoid these IRS red flags.
